commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Maintainer review is needed because the repair must choose the EPERM policy and atomicity boundary, and the contributor still needs to provide real Windows proof.; Needs attention: The diff changes permission-error handling in a filesystem-safety move primitive and still treats all Windows EPERM rename failures as recoverable.; Review finding: [P1] Keep non-lock Windows EPERM failures fail-closed — src/move-p... commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Windows EPERM handling to `movePathWithCopyFallback`, new source-backup restore logic, regression tests, and an Unreleased changelog entry.

Reproducibility: Source-level reproduction is clear: current main rethrows simulated EPERM from `guardedRename` because only EXDEV falls back. The real watched-directory Windows case and the after-fix behavior were not live-reproduced in this review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer review is needed because the repair must choose the EPERM policy and atomicity boundary, and the contributor still needs to provide real Windows proof.

target.title: fix: fall back to copy on EPERM during movePathWithCopyFallback
target.body:
## Problem

On Windows, when a process holds file watches on a directory (e.g. the OpenClaw Gateway watching the skills directory for hot-reload), `fs.rename` returns `EPERM` instead of `EXDEV`.

The current code only catches `EXDEV` to trigger the copy fallback, causing all skill updates to fail:

```
Error: EPERM: operation not permitted, rename '...' -> '.../.openclaw-install-backups/...'
```

## Fix

Add `EPERM` to the catch condition alongside `EXDEV`, so the copy fallback is also used when rename is denied due to file locking.

```typescript
// Before
if ((error as NodeJS.ErrnoException | null)?.code !== "EXDEV") {
// After  
const code = (error as NodeJS.ErrnoException | null)?.code;
if (code !== "EXDEV" && code !== "EPERM") {
```

## Why this is safe

The copy fallback (`copyEntryWithManifest` + `guardedRename`) is already implemented and handles directories correctly. On Windows, EPERM during rename is a common scenario (file locking by other processes) and should use the same fallback as EXDEV.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#88143
target.author: liuxingwei0601
